description: A cut may reveal 'lorika', the fluid that circulates through a living body. The same noun applies to blood in the body or a collected sample. Kinship remains 'thaluni' (kin) or another stated family relation rather than an English-style claim of shared blood.
articulatory_notes: Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for voiced 'l', let air pass along its sides, and draw the tongue back while rounding the lips for middle-height 'o'. Make a tap or short trill at the ridge and move into stressed high-front unrounded 'i'. Close the back of the tongue against the soft palate for unvoiced 'k', then release into open central unrounded 'a'.
usage_notes: The noun ordinarily treats blood as a substance. An exact type, amount, or laboratory result remains with its source record when that precision matters.

semantic_domains.physical: the fluid circulating through a living body
